note: Evals 1 and 3 have real side effects (assign the issue, move a board item, post a comment). Run only with the user present against a low-stakes issue.

evals.expected_output: Issue read in one gh call including comments; code surveyed read-only; a four-part plan (root cause, approach, risks, test plan) written to issue-<N>-plan.md; a housekeeping subagent assigns the issue, moves it to In Progress (if a board is configured), and posts the plan as an issue comment with the plan file marked as given/final in its prompt; after the subagent reports, the posted comment body is fetched via gh api and compared to the authored plan. No code is changed.

evals.prompt: what's the approach for #<N>? just want your read on it
evals.expected_output: The plan is delivered in chat only. The skill does NOT assign the issue, move the board, or post a comment without asking — a read-only request gets a read-only response, with an offer to post it.
Skill stops and asks the user before taking over an issue already assigned to someone else or already In Progress.
